title: Mitoguazone in vivo injections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ssce","text":"This protocol details a treatment paradigm using mitoguazone in vivo","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ftvfd","text":"EDTA coated tubes for blood collection. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"31c5m","text":"Syringes for weekly drug administration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q4df","text":"Scale (precision 0.1 g) to weight the pups.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ae2e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mice are injected with 2 mg/kg Mitoguazone, Sanbio BV Catalog #HY-106634/CS-0026193 (RRID: 67736) via subcutaneous injections at 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 weeks of age.
From 1 week of age, body weight of mice is monitored twice a week until 4 weeks of age, and once a week thereafter. Drinking water bottles are weighed weekly.
At 2, 6 and 10 weeks of age, mouse blood is collected via the submandibular vein in EDTA coated tubes (Sarstedt, 201288)
At 10 weeks of age, limb clasping, pole and open field tests are performed to assess the effect of the treatment on behavioral outcomes.
creator: Hanne HD Dhondt
